title: Frankincense and rosehip face serum
subtitle: A lightweight dry facial oil for mature or dry skin
excerpt: An anhydrous facial serum built on rosehip seed oil and jojoba oil, scented and enriched with frankincense essential oil. Rosehip is high in linoleic acid and trans-retinoic acid precursors; frankincense has a documented history of use in cosmetic preparations for older or dry skin.
sourceNotes: Rosehip seed oil composition and skin-care properties: USDA AOCS fatty acid database; D.F. Tarpley, Rosehip Seed Oil in Cosmetics (open-access cosmetic chemistry reference). Frankincense in cosmetic preparations: documented in Tisserand and Young, Essential Oil Safety (2nd ed., 2014). Safe facial usage rate for frankincense typically 0.5 to 1% of total formula weight.

recipe.yieldDescription: 1 dropper bottle (~30 ml, approximately 60 daily applications)
recipe.batchNotes: Scale proportionally. Keep essential oil content at 0.5 to 1% of total formula weight for a facial product.
recipe.makeAheadNotes: Ready to use immediately. Allow the bottle to reach room temperature if stored in the fridge before use.
recipe.shelfLifeNotes: Store in the dropper bottle in a cool, dark place or in the fridge to slow oxidation of the rosehip oil. Use within 12 months. Discard if the oil develops a sharp, paint-like smell (oxidised oil) or if colour darkens significantly.

glossaryTerms.definition: A preparation containing no water phase. Anhydrous serums and oils keep 12 months or more without a preservative because water-loving microbes have nothing to grow on.

An application of a small amount of a new cosmetic to the inside of the forearm. Leave for 24 to 48 hours before using the product on the face. This catches contact sensitisation before wider exposure.

content.text: Rosehip seed oil has one of the most studied profiles in cosmetic oil formulation. Its high linoleic acid content (40 to 50%) is important for skin-barrier function, and it also contains trans-retinoic acid precursors that contribute to its reputation for supporting cell turnover. It is a thin, dry-feeling oil that absorbs quickly without leaving a greasy film. Jojoba, technically a liquid wax, adds stability to the blend and further skin-compatible fatty acids. Frankincense essential oil contributes a resinous, slightly camphorous scent and has a long history of cosmetic use; at 0.5 to 1% of formula weight it is within the range considered suitable for facial use. This is an
preparation, so no preservative is needed. The formula makes 30 ml in a dropper bottle, roughly 60 daily applications if using 2 drops per use.

items.prepNote: 50% of formula; cold-pressed rosehip seed oil
40% of formula; golden or clear jojoba
6.5% of formula; antioxidant that slows rosehip oxidation

content.text: Weigh or measure each ingredient directly into the amber dropper bottle. Fit the dropper top and roll the bottle gently between the palms to combine; do not shake vigorously. The serum is ready to use immediately. If using a scale, work to 0.1 g precision; if using measuring spoons, 1 teaspoon is approximately 5 ml.
Before applying to the face, complete a
: apply 2 drops to the inside of the forearm and leave for 24 to 48 hours. No redness or irritation means the serum is suitable for facial use.

Apply 2 to 4 drops to clean, slightly damp skin in the morning or evening. Press gently into the face and neck rather than rubbing. A small amount goes a long way. Rosehip oil can stain pillowcases; apply in the morning or allow 10 minutes before lying down if using at night.

Store in the amber bottle in a cool dark place or in the fridge. The vitamin E oil slows oxidation but rosehip is one of the less stable cosmetic oils; a sharp, paint-like smell signals oxidation and the serum should then be discarded. Use within 12 months.
